user_query:
I’m preparing a methods session for my market fluidity and fiscal coverage project. Please set up a small, reproducible local analysis sandbox using only the provided files under input/ and produce a few deterministic artifacts. Do not use the Internet or any external services.

Required tasks and deliverables:
1) Environment and directories
- Read input/config.yaml to determine the output directories. Create these directories if they do not exist.
- Verify that input/market_liquidity.csv and input/fiscal_coverage.csv are present. Inspect the input/ directory and record basic file metadata.

2) Data checks and metrics (round all floating-point metrics to 4 decimals)
- Load both CSVs. Compute, from input/market_liquidity.csv:
  • overall row count (total_rows)
  • overall mean and median of bid_ask_spread (mean_spread, median_spread)
  • overall mean of depth (mean_depth)
- Join market_liquidity on input/fiscal_coverage.csv by date and compute the Pearson correlation between bid_ask_spread and coverage_ratio across all matched rows (name this spread_coverage_corr). Use all matched date rows (coverage_ratio repeats across markets by date).
- Compute per-market aggregates: avg_spread, avg_depth, and n_days (number of rows) for each market. Sort descending by avg_spread.

3) Artifacts to write to disk (paths from config):
- output/logs/input_manifest.json: for each input file (the two CSVs), include fields: file_path, size_bytes, row_count.
- output/metrics/summary_stats.csv: a single row with columns [total_rows, mean_spread, median_spread, mean_depth, spread_coverage_corr].
- output/metrics/market_aggregates.csv: columns [market, avg_spread, avg_depth, n_days], sorted by avg_spread descending.
- output/reports/meeting_notes.md: three sections with headings exactly as below:
  • Environment: include the Python version you ran with and a bullet list of the created output directories.
  • Key Metrics: include the numeric values from summary_stats and list the top 3 markets by avg_spread with their avg_spread values.
  • Action Items: include one bullet per market whose avg_spread exceeds the median of per-market avg_spread values; each bullet must start with "Investigate" and mention the market and its avg_spread value. End with a final bullet: "Confirm date alignment in fiscal coverage vs. liquidity data." 

Notes:
- All outputs must be written under the directories specified in input/config.yaml (e.g., output/metrics, output/logs, output/reports).
- Use only the local files provided; no external data or services.
- The task will be considered complete if all four output files are created with consistent numbers derived from the inputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
